packet.c(3) Library Functions Manual packet.c(3)

NAME

packet.c

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t packet_send (socket_t sockfd, packet_type_t type, const void *data, size_t len)
Send a packet with proper header and CRC32. asciichat_error_t packet_receive (socket_t sockfd, packet_type_t *type, void **data, size_t *len)
Receive a packet with proper header validation and CRC32 checking. asciichat_error_t send_packet_secure (socket_t sockfd, packet_type_t type, const void *data, size_t len, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Send a packet with encryption and compression support. packet_recv_result_t receive_packet_secure (socket_t sockfd, void *crypto_ctx, bool enforce_encryption, packet_envelope_t *envelope)
Receive a packet with decryption and decompression support. int send_packet (socket_t sockfd, packet_type_t type, const void *data, size_t len)
Send a basic packet without encryption. int receive_packet (socket_t sockfd, packet_type_t *type, void **data, size_t *len)
Receive a basic packet without encryption. int send_ping_packet (socket_t sockfd)
Send a ping packet. int send_pong_packet (socket_t sockfd)
Send a pong packet. asciichat_error_t packet_decrypt_envelope (packet_envelope_t *envelope, void *crypto_ctx)
Send a clear console packet. asciichat_error_t packet_send_error (socket_t sockfd, const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx, asciichat_error_t error_code, const char *message)
Send an error packet with optional encryption context. asciichat_error_t packet_parse_error_message (const void *data, size_t len, asciichat_error_t *out_error_code, char *message_buffer, size_t message_buffer_size, size_t *out_message_length)
Parse an error packet payload into components. asciichat_error_t packet_send_remote_log (socket_t sockfd, const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx, log_level_t level, remote_log_direction_t direction, uint16_t flags, const char *message)
Send a remote log packet with optional encryption context. asciichat_error_t packet_parse_remote_log (const void *data, size_t len, log_level_t *out_level, remote_log_direction_t *out_direction, uint16_t *out_flags, char *message_buffer, size_t message_buffer_size, size_t *out_message_length)
Parse a remote log packet payload into components. int send_protocol_version_packet (socket_t sockfd, const protocol_version_packet_t *version)
Send protocol version packet. int send_crypto_capabilities_packet (socket_t sockfd, const crypto_capabilities_packet_t *caps)
Send crypto capabilities packet. int send_crypto_parameters_packet (socket_t sockfd, const crypto_parameters_packet_t *params)
Send crypto parameters packet. asciichat_error_t av_send_audio_opus_batch (socket_t sockfd, const uint8_t *opus_data, size_t opus_size, const uint16_t *frame_sizes, int sample_rate, int frame_duration, int frame_count, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Send Opus-encoded audio batch packet with encryption support. asciichat_error_t send_image_frame_packet (socket_t sockfd, const void *image_data, uint16_t width, uint16_t height, uint8_t format)
Send image frame packet.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t packet_parse_error_message (const void * data, size_t len, asciichat_error_t * out_error_code, char * message_buffer, size_t message_buffer_size, size_t * out_message_length)

Parse an error packet payload into components.

Parameters

data Packet payload buffer
len Payload length in bytes
out_error_code Output pointer for asciichat_error_t value (must not be NULL)
message_buffer Destination buffer for message string (must not be NULL)
message_buffer_size Size of destination buffer in bytes (must be > 0)
out_message_length Optional output for message length reported by sender

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Definition at line 942 of file packet.c.

944                                                                          {
945   if (!data || len < sizeof(error_packet_t) || !out_error_code || !message_buffer || message_buffer_size == 0) {
946     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
947                      "Invalid parameters: data=%p len=%zu out_error_code=%p message_buffer=%p buffer_size=%zu", data,
948                      len, out_error_code, message_buffer, message_buffer_size);
949   }
950 
951   const error_packet_t *packet = (const error_packet_t *)data;
952   uint32_t raw_error_code = NET_TO_HOST_U32(packet->error_code);
953   uint32_t raw_message_length = NET_TO_HOST_U32(packet->message_length);
954 
955   if (raw_message_length > MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH) {
956     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Error message length too large: %u", raw_message_length);
957   }
958 
959   size_t total_required = sizeof(error_packet_t) + (size_t)raw_message_length;
960   if (total_required > len) {
961     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Error packet truncated: expected %zu bytes, have %zu", total_required,
962                      len);
963   }
964 
965   const uint8_t *message_bytes = (const uint8_t *)data + sizeof(error_packet_t);
966   size_t copy_len = raw_message_length;
967   if (copy_len >= message_buffer_size) {
968     copy_len = message_buffer_size - 1;
969   }
970 
971   if (copy_len > 0) {
972     memcpy(message_buffer, message_bytes, copy_len);
973   }
974   message_buffer[copy_len] = '\0';
975 
976   if (out_message_length) {
977     *out_message_length = raw_message_length;
978   }
979 
980   *out_error_code = (asciichat_error_t)raw_error_code;
981   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
982 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK, error_packet_t::error_code,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
    MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH, error_packet_t::message_length,
    NET_TO_HOST_U32, and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t packet_parse_remote_log (const void *
  data, size_t len, log_level_t * out_level,
  remote_log_direction_t * out_direction, uint16_t * out_flags,
  char * message_buffer, size_t message_buffer_size, size_t *
  out_message_length)


Parse a remote log packet payload into components.


Parameters


data Packet payload buffer


len Payload length in bytes


out_level Output pointer for log level (must not be NULL)


out_direction Output pointer for direction (must not be NULL)


out_flags Output pointer for flags (must not be NULL)


message_buffer Destination buffer for message string (must not be NULL)


message_buffer_size Size of destination buffer in bytes (must be > 0)


out_message_length Optional output for message length reported by
  sender


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 1041 of file packet.c.


1044                                                                       {
1045   if (!data || len < sizeof(remote_log_packet_t) || !out_level || !out_direction || !out_flags || !message_buffer ||
1046       message_buffer_size == 0) {
1047     return SET_ERRNO(
1048         ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1049         "Invalid parameters: data=%p len=%zu out_level=%p out_direction=%p out_flags=%p buffer=%p size=%zu", data, len,
1050         out_level, out_direction, out_flags, message_buffer, message_buffer_size);
1051   }
1052 
1053   const remote_log_packet_t *packet = (const remote_log_packet_t *)data;
1054   uint8_t raw_level = packet->log_level;
1055   if (raw_level > LOG_FATAL) {
1056     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid remote log level: %u", raw_level);
1057   }
1058   *out_level = (log_level_t)raw_level;
1059 
1060   uint8_t raw_direction = packet->direction;
1061   if (raw_direction > REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_CLIENT_TO_SERVER) {
1062     raw_direction = REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_UNKNOWN;
1063   }
1064   *out_direction = (remote_log_direction_t)raw_direction;
1065 
1066   uint16_t raw_flags = NET_TO_HOST_U16(packet->flags);
1067   *out_flags = raw_flags;
1068 
1069   uint32_t raw_message_length = NET_TO_HOST_U32(packet->message_length);
1070   if (raw_message_length > MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH) {
1071     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Remote log message length too large: %u", raw_message_length);
1072   }
1073 
1074   size_t total_required = sizeof(remote_log_packet_t) + (size_t)raw_message_length;
1075   if (total_required > len) {
1076     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Remote log packet truncated: expected %zu bytes, have %zu",
1077                      total_required, len);
1078   }
1079 
1080   const uint8_t *message_bytes = (const uint8_t *)data + sizeof(remote_log_packet_t);
1081   size_t copy_len = raw_message_length;
1082   if (copy_len >= message_buffer_size) {
1083     copy_len = message_buffer_size - 1;
1084   }
1085 
1086   if (copy_len > 0) {
1087     memcpy(message_buffer, message_bytes, copy_len);
1088   }
1089   message_buffer[copy_len] = '\0';
1090 
1091   if (out_message_length) {
1092     *out_message_length = raw_message_length;
1093   }
1094 
1095   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1096 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    remote_log_packet_t::direction, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, remote_log_packet_t::flags,
    LOG_FATAL, remote_log_packet_t::log_level,
    MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH,
    remote_log_packet_t::message_length, NET_TO_HOST_U16,
    NET_TO_HOST_U32, REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_CLIENT_TO_SERVER,
    REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_UNKNOWN, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by handle_remote_log_packet_from_client().






asciichat_error_t packet_send_error (socket_t
  sockfd, const crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx, asciichat_error_t
  error_code, const char * message)


Send an error packet with optional encryption context.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


crypto_ctx Crypto context for encryption (NULL or not ready sends
  plaintext)


error_code Error code from
  asciichat_error_t enumeration


message Human-readable message to accompany the error (can be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


When the crypto context is ready, the packet is encrypted
    automatically. During the handshake (or when encryption is disabled), the
    packet is sent in plaintext so protocol errors can be delivered before
    encryption is active.


Definition at line 895 of file packet.c.


896                                                          {
897   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
898     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid socket descriptor");
899   }
900 
901   if (!message) {
902     message = "";
903   }
904 
905   size_t message_len = strnlen(message, MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH);
906   if (message_len == MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH) {
907     log_warn("Error message truncated to %zu bytes", (size_t)MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH);
908   }
909 
910   size_t payload_len = sizeof(error_packet_t) + message_len;
911   uint8_t *payload = SAFE_MALLOC(payload_len, uint8_t *);
912   if (!payload) {
913     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate %zu bytes for error packet", payload_len);
914   }
915 
916   error_packet_t *packet = (error_packet_t *)payload;
917   packet->error_code = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)error_code);
918   packet->message_length = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)message_len);
919 
920   if (message_len > 0) {
921     memcpy(payload + sizeof(error_packet_t), message, message_len);
922   }
923 
924   bool encryption_ready = crypto_ctx && crypto_is_ready(crypto_ctx);
925   asciichat_error_t send_result;
926 
927   if (encryption_ready) {
928     send_result =
929         send_packet_secure(sockfd, PACKET_TYPE_ERROR_MESSAGE, payload, payload_len, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
930   } else {
931     send_result = packet_send(sockfd, PACKET_TYPE_ERROR_MESSAGE, payload, payload_len);
932   }
933   SAFE_FREE(payload);
934 
935   if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
936     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send error packet: %s", asciichat_error_string(send_result));
937   }
938 
939   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
940 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, crypto_is_ready(),
    error_code, error_packet_t::error_code,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    HOST_TO_NET_U32, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, log_warn,
    MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH, error_packet_t::message_length,
    packet_send(), PACKET_TYPE_ERROR_MESSAGE, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, send_packet_secure(), and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by disconnect_client_for_bad_data().






asciichat_error_t packet_send_remote_log (socket_t
  sockfd, const crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx, log_level_t level,
  remote_log_direction_t direction, uint16_t flags, const char *
  message)


Send a remote log packet with optional encryption context.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


crypto_ctx Crypto context for encryption (NULL or not ready sends
  plaintext)


level Log level to transmit


direction Direction flag (REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_*)


flags Additional flags (REMOTE_LOG_FLAG_*)


message Log message text


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 984 of file packet.c.


985                                                                                                                 {
986   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
987     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid socket descriptor");
988   }
989 
990   if (level < LOG_DEV || level > LOG_FATAL) {
991     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid log level: %d", level);
992   }
993 
994   const char *safe_message = message ? message : "";
995   bool truncated = false;
996   size_t message_len = strnlen(safe_message, MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH);
997   if (message_len == MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH && safe_message[message_len] != '\0') {
998     truncated = true;
999   }
1000 
1001   size_t payload_len = sizeof(remote_log_packet_t) + message_len;
1002   uint8_t *payload = SAFE_MALLOC(payload_len, uint8_t *);
1003   if (!payload) {
1004     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate %zu bytes for remote log packet", payload_len);
1005   }
1006 
1007   remote_log_packet_t *packet = (remote_log_packet_t *)payload;
1008   packet->log_level = (uint8_t)level;
1009   packet->direction = (uint8_t)direction;
1010   uint16_t final_flags = flags;
1011   if (truncated) {
1012     final_flags |= REMOTE_LOG_FLAG_TRUNCATED;
1013   }
1014   packet->flags = HOST_TO_NET_U16(final_flags);
1015   packet->message_length = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)message_len);
1016 
1017   if (message_len > 0) {
1018     memcpy(payload + sizeof(remote_log_packet_t), safe_message, message_len);
1019   }
1020 
1021   bool encryption_ready = crypto_ctx && crypto_is_ready(crypto_ctx);
1022   asciichat_error_t send_result;
1023 
1024   if (encryption_ready) {
1025     int secure_result =
1026         send_packet_secure(sockfd, PACKET_TYPE_REMOTE_LOG, payload, payload_len, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
1027     send_result = secure_result == 0 ? ASCIICHAT_OK : ERROR_NETWORK;
1028   } else {
1029     send_result = packet_send(sockfd, PACKET_TYPE_REMOTE_LOG, payload, payload_len);
1030   }
1031 
1032   SAFE_FREE(payload);
1033 
1034   if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1035     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send remote log packet: %d", send_result);
1036   }
1037 
1038   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1039 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, crypto_is_ready(),
    remote_log_packet_t::direction, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    remote_log_packet_t::flags, HOST_TO_NET_U16,
    HOST_TO_NET_U32, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, LOG_FATAL,
    remote_log_packet_t::log_level, MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH,
    remote_log_packet_t::message_length, packet_send(),
    PACKET_TYPE_REMOTE_LOG, REMOTE_LOG_FLAG_TRUNCATED,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, send_packet_secure(), and
    SET_ERRNO.
